chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D-1049 of 2023 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objection at flag `A` 2. For hearing of main case 16-05-2024 Mr. Shabbir Ali Bozdar, Advocate for the Petitioner Mr. Agha Ather Hussain Pathan, Assistant Advocate General Asif Khan Khoso, Inspector Excise and Taxation, Sukkur ASI Ghulam Akbar of PS A-Section Khairpur.-.-.-. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- O R D E R Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro,J:- Learned AAG has filed comments of respondent No.7. Asif Khan Khoso, Inspector Excise & Taxation Sukkur is present and has confirmed that against the name of petitioner six motor vehicles are registered. He further submits that there is a provision in law stipulating that if a vehicle is registered wrongly in the name of unrelated person and his/her CNIC has been misused, he / she shall get such entry recorded in the daily diary with the relevant police station and cause publication of a notice in different Newspapers disowning purchasing vehicle(s) and claiming misuse of his / her CNIC. Then he /she shall submit a copy of such daily diary and publication with Excise & Taxation Department, upon which in the remakes against the registration of vehicles “safe custody” would be marked which would mean that as and when such vehicle is found plying on the road, it shall be taken into custody and proceedings be initiated for either cancelling the registration in the name of wrong person or transferring it to the person having possession thereof in accordance with law. Learned counsel for the petitioner has acknowledged this procedure as requirement of law and submits that he would comply with it and pursue the case accordingly by moving an application to the relevant police Station for recording entry and making such publication in Newspapers, and approaching thereafter to the Excise Office Sukkur for acting accordingly.

Then he /she shall submit a copy of such daily diary and publication with Excise & Taxation Department, upon which in the remakes against the registration of vehicles “safe custody” would be marked which would mean that as and when such vehicle is found plying on the road, it shall be taken into custody and proceedings be initiated for either cancelling the registration in the name of wrong person or transferring it to the person having possession thereof in accordance with law. Learned counsel for the petitioner has acknowledged this procedure as requirement of law and submits that he would comply with it and pursue the case accordingly by moving an application to the relevant police Station for recording entry and making such publication in Newspapers, and approaching thereafter to the Excise Office Sukkur for acting accordingly. Consequently, this petition is disposed of in above terms. All the parties are required to act as mandated by law and submit such report after the whole exercise is completed for a perusal in chamber. JUDGE JUDGE Sulemen Khan/PA 2
